name: 2D & Nanomaterials
what: Graphene and borophene with exceptional properties.
how: Atom-thin layers enable new electronics and structures.

name: Advanced Composites
what: Lightweight high-strength materials for transport.
how: Fibre-reinforced matrices tuned for stiffness and weight.

name: Metamaterials
what: Engineered structures with properties not found in nature.
how: Sub-wavelength patterns bend light and sound unusually.

name: MAX Phase Ceramics
use: High-performance ceramics for electronics and aerospace applications.
what: Developed from layered crystal structures with unique properties.
how: Offers improved thermal stability, mechanical strength, and chemical resistance.

name: Topological Insulators
what: Materials that conduct electricity on their surface but not through their interior.
how: Utilize unique properties for advanced electronics and quantum computing applications.

name: 2D Materials Beyond Graphene (Borophene, Silicene)
what: Borophene and silicene are two-dimensional materials with potential applications in electronics and energy storage.
how: Researches the unique properties of these materials to develop new technologies.

name: Borophene
what: A two-dimensional material with exceptional electrical conductivity, making it ideal for next-generation electronics.
how: Utilizes unique electronic properties to enhance device performance and efficiency.

name: Programmable Matter
what: A technology allowing materials to change shape and properties based on external stimuli.
how: Uses advanced polymers and nanotechnology to create adaptive structures.

name: MAX Phase Ceramics for High-Temperature Applications
what: Exceptional thermal stability and mechanical strength.
how: Ideal for high-temperature applications.

name: Topological Insulators for Quantum Computing
what: Conduct electricity only on surfaces.
how: Promising materials for qubits.

name: 4D Printing Materials
what: Capable of self-assembling into complex shapes and structures.
how: Could revolutionize the way we design and produce products.

name: Topological Insulators, 2D Materials Beyond Graphene (Borophene, Silicene)
use: Advanced electronic and optical properties for next-generation devices
what: Novel materials with unique topological properties that can be used in electronics and photonics.
how: Researching and developing 2D materials like borophene and silicene for their exceptional electrical and optical characteristics.

name: Programmable Matter, 4D Printing Materials
use: Dynamic materials that can change shape and properties on demand
what: Advanced materials capable of morphing into different forms or functions based on external stimuli.
how: Creating programmable matter with the ability to transform in response to environmental changes, enabling new applications in manufacturing and construction.

name: Topological Insulators in Electronics
what: Using topological insulators to develop new electronic devices.
how: Improving device performance and reducing power consumption.

name: Programmable Matter for Smart Structures
what: Developing materials that can change shape and properties in response to external stimuli.
how: Enabling smart buildings and infrastructure with adaptive features.

name: Silicene
what: Two-dimensional material similar to graphene but made of silicon atoms.
how: Potential applications in electronics and optoelectronics.

name: MAX Phase Ceramics (NOW)
what: Unique thermal and mechanical properties for high-temperature environments.
how: Oak Ridge National Laboratory, GE Addiion

name: Topological Insulators (NEAR)
what: Materials that conduct electricity on their surface while insulating in the bulk.
how: University of California, Berkeley, MIT

name: 2D Materials Beyond Graphene (Borophene, Silicene) (FAR)
what: Two-dimensional materials with unique properties.
how: Rice University, IBM Research

name: Programmable Matter and Shape-Shifting Materials
what: Materials that can change their shape or properties based on external stimuli.
how: Development of new polymers and smart materials for various applications.

name: 4D Printing Materials for Structural Integrity and Adaptability
what: Advanced materials capable of changing shape over time to adapt to environmental conditions.
